item.title: Re: I love it!
item.content:encoded: Thank you. I really can't articulate how wonderful it is to read this.

item.title: Re: I love it!
item.content:encoded: I couldn’t agree more. This is absolutely (in my opinion) one of the best series I SCPs out there - it’s an excellent example of how to embrace simplicity as a means to make each sentence important. It’s short but effective, and each sentence feels significant in bringing us to this final, emotional ending note. What a piece.

item.title: I love it!
item.content:encoded: This work is delicate and moving, with a sense of sadness, but also with a sense of beauty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I completely agree with the 1920s vibe. Especially since 1920s were the time when cigarettes were considered “cool” and became popular among women. The whole atmosphere surrounding this scp is great

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of my favourite Series I SCPs, purely because it's so simple yet has a lot of room for the reader's imagination to run wild. Perhaps cross-testing logs would be cool to see with other SCPs? +1 anyway.

Also, I think I see a grammatical error in the containment procedures - It reads "SCP-013 are contained…". However, the first line of the description reads "SCP-013 is the collective designation for…" - would it not make more grammatical sense for the containment protocols to instead read "SCP-013 is contained…"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
013, and you’re pretty unlucky if you smoke one of these by accident.
Or not. Depends on the person.
This is the first (numerically) article to include a suicide note. Ignoring the realism of including a note like this in your report, I feel like suicide notes become a bit oveeused later on, but this is still early. A lot of thenm are just excuses to dump backstory too, so I’m glad this avoids that.
Otherwise, I’m not too invested. It does it’s job fine.
+0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple, fun, and pretty dang neat. One of the few scips I'd actually want to own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of my favorite series 1 skips. I'm not sure if this is what Dexanote intended, but I get sort of a 1920s vibe from this. With the image of the woman, the name "Blue Lady Cigarettes", and just the general tone of it makes my mind think of some somber blues tunes, like Billie Holiday style. It made me feel something, and it shows that not all skips have to be scary to be great. +1

item.title: Re: Image Update
item.content:encoded: Symbolic containment. The object is encapsulated within the boundaries of the symbol and therefore any properties bound by magical law are contained.

item.title: Re: Image update
item.content:encoded:
While I don't think it's "blue lady" looks handwritten, but they're not normal cigarettes.

That's a cigarette that makes you think you're turning into a woman when you're actually not.
Normal cigarettes doesn't make you think you're becoming a woman, let alone actually changing you.

item.title: Image update
item.content:encoded:
Now wait just a minute Isn't the cigarette A normal cigarette With a blue HANDWRITTEN label
Sorry if I sound mean that's all

item.title: Re: Image Update
item.content:encoded: Gotta make it look good for the SCP Latest Anomaly Magazine.

item.title: Re: Image Update
item.content:encoded: Now I will ask myself why did the Foundation have tables engraved with their symbols?

item.title: Re: Image Update
item.content:encoded: A bit late, but correct! I like this picture quite a bit. While it's a bit excessive (and the old picture was fine CC wise, as I took it), I like the effort a LOT and this is gorgeous and works perfectly for an Early Series 1 introductory picture.

item.title: Image Update
item.content:encoded:
I updated the image yesterday with permission from the author, Dexanote (thank you!)

The updated image was created using the following Wikimedia Creative Commons image as the base, and modified over several nights to accurately fit the SCP.
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cigarette-s_x.jpeg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, I hope the author updates with crosstesting logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem! Did you know, by the by, that there's a new-ish reading of this skip here? I quite liked the emotion in the last note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. Thank you! I'm happy you like it enough for it to be a favourite!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The note really drives it home- I can say this is one of my all-time favourites down to the simplistic yet strong narrative it presents. I really, really like this object.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Swell! WikiWalk Proposal post edited.

You're the best Dexanote!

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Included an uninvasive crosslink to 1890.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
"And Related Evidence" would suggest that 1890 came from the same apartment, and I'm not comfortable retconning another authors SCP in that kind of implied way.

Its 413 AM where I am but now that this was brought to my attention again, I'd like to try to connect 013 to 1890. I'll come up with a satisfying link tomorrow afternoon when I'm done other pre-Christmas stuff.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Well, that works too. Assuming Dex's okay with it, the inline link to 1890 seems like a solid first choice of crosslink.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I'm a fan of SCP-2438, but I'm not sure it's the right link here. The tone is quite different, for a start.

Having had a brief look for "mind-affecting" / "metamorphic" SCPs, I wonder whether this could be linked to SCP-1890? That is another skip involving vivid perception of mysterious persons, with no eventual explanation. It also has the advantage of existing further links to SCPs 1896 and 1928.

The simplest solution would be an inline edit to SCP-1890 from the phrase "and related evidence" in the last paragraph before the addendum. There's no need to give any further explanation - SCP-1890 doesn't have recovery details, and readers can draw their own conclusions.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Author made edits. See discussion below.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

This is another one of those Series 1 articles that's both very brief, and has a largely self contained narrative. If anything, a crosslink that would work would be the Foundation checking to see what the limits on the memetic effect of the cigarettes are, and if there is any way to reverse the process of their effects. Luckily, SCP-2438 exists. A rather recent SCP, the rabbits there in produce an incredibly powerful amnestic, one that the Foundation might test to see if its effective against 013's effect. As such, I am proposing the following edit:

Changing the following:

Immediately after consuming an instance of SCP-013, subjects will gradually begin to perceive reflections of themselves as having the features of the woman, and will gradually perceive their bodies changing to reflect her appearance over the course of the following weeks. All changes are entirely mental; the subject’s body does not change outwardly, only their perception of themselves. These alterations are permanent, and cannot be reverse.

To this:

Immediately after consuming an instance of SCP-013, subjects will gradually begin to perceive reflections of themselves as having the features of the woman, and will gradually perceive their bodies changing to reflect her appearance over the course of the following weeks. All changes are entirely mental; the subject’s body does not change outwardly, only their perception of themselves. These alterations are permanent, and cannot be reversed through any known means. Products from Project Lapinate have been proposed as a potential reversal method, with tests pending Project Lapinate's full approval.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: Re: Question
item.content:encoded: It doesn't change your body. It changes your body IMAGE. That means the ONLY thing that changes is how you see and feel your own body. Everyone else still sees your normal, boring, not-Blue-Lady body

item.title: Question
item.content:encoded: I wonder though, do you FEEL the changes? Like would you feel your body differently than before?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just goes to show that an SCP doesn't have to be full out horror scary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y4QGPWLY-EM

I could not get this song out of my head while reading this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: no

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, do these things make you high, too?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The edit comment of the last edit states "Removed Addednum note until I can redo it in a more satisfactory manner."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article says there is a transcription of a note at the bottom, but there isn't. Am I missing something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough. My upvote stays, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can accept that it was there before, but I have to evaluate and vote on the article as it stands, not based on a previous revision. As it stands, it's a downvote - when the addendum is put back, it would at best be a neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Removed Addednum note until I can redo it in a more satisfactory manner.

The above is the text of the note on the last page revision. If you look at the page revision before that, you'll find a note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't articulate why this one doesn't grab me enough for an upvote.

I can articulate why I downvoted: You mention a note "transcribed below", but do not either include the note or mention that the contents were redacted. It's a loose element that annoys me rather than pull me in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was always the appeal for me as well. Call it shallow, but I'd love to be under this skip's effects. Certainly the kind of body I'm looking for if I'm having a hard time transitioning :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this one.
Maybe because I'm a transwoman?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I have the image of a big male D-Class trying to escape through a vent, and it's hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if a 200 kg person smokes it? I'd love to see what situations could result from thinking you're 1/4 of your actual weight (trying to fit through narrow doors, for example). Would he perceive it as being blocked by some force?

item.title: Re: Admin Post (Closed)
item.content:encoded: Just going to take a chance to say that I removed that line because I didn't like how it looked any more. No harm no foul. Carry on.

item.title: Admin Post (Closed)
item.content:encoded:
Since this is on a separate page, please see here.

Do not reply to this post.

item.title: Admin Post (Closed)
item.content:encoded:
The subthread comprising the discussion of Agent_beanners arguing with staff (and in support of their edit) is now closed.

Additionally, Agent_beanners, you're violating the Site Rules, which state:

You may question the actions of other users and staff as long as it is done in a calm, mature fashion.

No more discussion of Agent_beanner's edit (unless it comes from Dexanote) or Agent_beanner's personal life in this thread, please.

Do not reply to this post. Feel free to send a PM to myself or Zyn if you have more to say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah you"re probably right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry to hear it, chief. Maybe take some time to calm down, then. You don't want to do something dumb out of anger and get in trouble for it.

I'd take some time off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah sorry my grandpa just died recently and my girlfriend broke up with me along with some personnel stuff i don't fell comfortable saying so i guess i"m not in a good mood.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off, when replying to another user's comment, you can just hit "Reply" under the comment instead of "New Post". I assume you already knew that since you've done it once, but friendly reminder anyway since doing that helps keep things organized when you're reading through threads.

Second of all, here's the deal. It is Dexanote's article, and if Dexanote wants to make a change, Dexanote will make a change. It isn't your place to say what or what isn't an error, that's up to him. Certain obvious things, like grammar, spelling, etc? Sure. If it isn't otherwise stated that it was intentional, go right ahead. But edits like this? Totally within the realm of an author's decision, and as such, it isn't up to us to decide what to do with it.

Lastly, and this is more of a personal issue than anything, I would hesitate being comfortable with somebody making edits to other people's articles when you're not willing to use correct spelling/grammar/etc. in the forums. Before I hear "but djkaktus it's the internet and it's just the forums what does it matter", it matters because by editing another person's article like this, you're making an executive decision on another person's piece of work from the position of an author. That means something, and so if you then turn around and muck around with language in a forum thread no less, then how does that look to the people who take care of the articles, or to the author?

Doesn't look good, Jack. Doesn't look good at all.

Note: As an aside, I wouldn't backtalk Zyn. She's the single nicest person on the wiki and was trying to do you a favor by pointing out a mistake that was made. Plenty of people would have been more than willing to come down harder, and would've been well within their right.

Edit: Noticed your reply. You done goofed, brother. Ain't the right attitude to have around here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT can a mod or admin delete this please

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you're continuing a conversation, you can click the "reply" link at the lower right of the comment you're responding to. Helps so people are sure of who you're talking to. If you make a new comment, forum-goers assume you're talking to everyone reading the thread, not necessarily in reply to someone.

Furthermore, it was not a clear error, and it's likely no one "noticed" because it was the author who made the edit. Others on the site know to let the author do what they will with their writing; the pieces are effectively their property.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Soooo what i got from your post is that i should not edit clear errors even when no one has noticed them. THAT makes perfect sense! EDIT And you also expect me to always assume that obvious errors are ALWAYS deliberately made by the author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can compare page sources using the "History" link at the bottom of each page. Please do not make assumptions about authorial intent, especially if the author is an active member on the site and easy to contact (Dexanote himself is a site moderator).

From now on, please make a post in the discussion threads or contact a staff member prior to editing pages yourself.

And beside why would dexanote want to change the note like that when it dosen"t even make any sense?

You should have asked him before assuming it was a mistake. I will contact him myself on chat, and leave him a message asking that he weigh in on the change. If it turns out it was a mistake, then he'll fix it himself. That's something that falls within the author's responsibility.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was revision 16 and i assumed that dexanote fixed something like the grammar (or something like that) and accidentally messed up the last line in the note. EDIT And beside why would dexanote want to change the note like that when it dosen"t even make any sense?

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
For the record, I am a site moderator. Be assured that I read your edit note, and the revision stands.

Edit: It appears that the change was made by Dexanote, who is the original author of the piece. Please respect the author's wishes and be more careful with your edits from now on.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Just to be clear i fixed the part of the note where it said her favorite flavor and when i first read the article i clearly remember it saying she left me her favorite flavor so i assumed that it was a error and fixed it.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent change was reverted, as it was a major stylistic addition that was not approved by the author or staff.

Grammatical edits are appreciated, but readers should remember to consider an author's original intent before making edits. As such, while punctuation and spelling can be corrected, keep in mind that style points should remain as they are and substantial changes to content should not be made without authorial permission (which can be obtained by asking either in the forums or in PM).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reluctantly no-voting.
